stories.title: Cardinals 6, Cubs 5 — Velázquez Callup Stings in Game 1
stories.angle: Game recap. Nelson Velázquez, promoted from Triple-A Memphis earlier that day, hit a 3-run HR to key the Cardinals' comeback. Riley O'Brien recorded the save. Cubs drop Game 1 at Busch, fall to 31-27.

x_posts.label: Post 1A
x_posts.text:
Cardinals 6, Cubs 5. A callup from Memphis ended the Cubs' winning streak.

Nelson Velázquez — promoted from Triple-A that day — hit a 3-run HR to key the comeback. Riley O'Brien finished it off. Cubs drop Game 1 at Busch, fall to 31-27.

#Cubs #GoCubs #MLB

research.facts:
Cardinals 6, Cubs 5 — Velázquez Callup Stings in Game 1
**Facts:**
- [HIGH] Final score: Cardinals 6, Cubs 5. Sources: ESPN (gameId 401815542), CBS Chicago, Washington Post
- [HIGH] Nelson Velázquez hit a 3-run HR. Sources: CBS Chicago + Washington Post
- [HIGH] Velázquez was promoted from Triple-A Memphis earlier on May 29. Sources: CBS Chicago
- [MEDIUM] First MLB action for Velázquez since June 23, 2024 (last with Kansas City). Source: CBS Chicago (single source)
- [HIGH] Cardinals snapped a 4-game losing streak. Sources: CBS Chicago + ESPN context

- [MEDIUM] Riley O'Brien recorded the save (cited as "14th save"). Source: CBS Chicago only
- [MEDIUM] Iván Herrera solo HR. Source: Washington Post
- [MEDIUM] Thomas Saggese solo HR. Source: Washington Post
- [HIGH] Cubs entered with back-to-back wins (from Pittsburgh series)
- [HIGH] Cubs fall to 31-27 after this loss (derived from series-context, which showed 31-27)

stories.title: Cardinals Snapped 4-Game Losing Streak — On the Cubs' Dime
stories.angle: Bold take. Cardinals' relief corps (Graceffo win, O'Brien save) shut down the Cubs without a marquee arm. St. Louis didn't need a star to beat Chicago — and that's the real problem.

x_posts.label: Post 2A
x_posts.text:
Cardinals snapped a 4-game losing streak on the Cubs' dime — Gordon Graceffo won it, Riley O'Brien saved it.

St. Louis didn't need a marquee performance. Cubs fall to 31-27 and can't afford another slip in this series.

#Cubs #GoCubs #CubsBaseball

- [MEDIUM] Riley O'Brien recorded the save (CBS Chicago only)
- [HIGH] Cubs had momentum entering: won 2 straight before series (Pirates)

stories.title: Ben Brown vs. Kyle Leahy — Game 2 Tonight at 6:15 PM CT
stories.angle: Game 2 preview. Brown has a 2.01 ERA and 47 K in 44.2 IP — one of the most underrated starters in the NL. Leahy counters at 4.44 ERA. Cubs have the pitching matchup advantage.

x_posts.label: Post 3A
x_posts.text:
Ben Brown tonight: 2.01 ERA, 0.99 WHIP, 47 K in 44.2 IP — quietly one of the best starters in the NL.

Kyle Leahy counters for St. Louis at 4.44 ERA. The pitching matchup favors Chicago. First pitch: 6:15 PM CT at Busch.

#Cubs #GoCubs #MLB

stories.title: NL Central Reckoning — Cubs Trail Cardinals, 4.5 Back of Brewers
stories.angle: Standings analysis. Brewers 33-20, Cardinals 30-25, Cubs 31-27. Cubs have slipped 0.5 GB behind St. Louis in the standings and are 4.5 back of Milwaukee. Tonight matters.

x_posts.label: Post 4A
x_posts.text:
NL Central update: Brewers 33-20, Cardinals 30-25, Cubs 31-27.

Cubs have slipped 0.5 GB behind St. Louis in the standings. A loss tonight puts Chicago 1.5 back of the Cardinals with just 1 game left in this series. Tonight is not optional.

#Cubs #NorthSiders #MLB

research.facts:
NL Central Reckoning — Cubs Trail Cardinals, 4.5 Back of Brewers
**Facts:**
- [HIGH] Cubs record: 31-27 (derived from series-context — was 31-26, lost Game 1)
- [HIGH] Cardinals record: 30-25 (series-context.json)
- [MEDIUM] Brewers record: 33-20 (FanDuel research, single source)
- [HIGH] Cubs GB behind Cardinals: 0.5 GB (math: ((30-31) + (27-25)) / 2 = 0.5)
- [MEDIUM] Cubs GB behind Brewers: 4.5 GB (math: ((33-31) + (27-20)) / 2 = 4.5 — requires Brewers 33-20 being correct)
- [HIGH] A Cubs loss tonight would put them 1.5 GB behind Cardinals (math: 31-28 vs 30-25 = ((30-31) + (28-25)) / 2 = 1.5)
**Sources:**
- Series-context.json (Cubs + Cardinals records)
- FanDuel research (Brewers record)

stories.title: Boyd Set to Return; Wicks Progressing in Iowa Rehab
stories.angle: Rotation health update. Boyd (left meniscus) set to be activated this week. Wicks throwing 3 scoreless innings in Iowa rehab. With Horton and Steele still sidelined, rotation depth is building.

x_posts.label: Post 5A
x_posts.text:
Matthew Boyd is set to return to the Cubs rotation this week after his left meniscus injury.

While Horton and Steele remain out, Boyd's activation and Wicks' 3 scoreless Iowa rehab innings give this rotation real depth heading into June.

#Cubs #GoCubs #CubsBaseball

research.facts:
Boyd Set to Return; Wicks Progressing in Iowa Rehab
**Facts:**
- [HIGH] Boyd on 15-day IL with left meniscus injury (CBS Sports + FanGraphs — 2+ sources)
- [MEDIUM] Boyd "being activated to start Wednesday's Phillies game." Sources: FanGraphs, CBS Sports (both reference Wednesday activation — MEDIUM because "Wednesday" is referenced specifically but exact date not confirmed independently)
- [MEDIUM] Jordan Wicks: 3 scoreless innings in Iowa rehab (1 H, 1 BB, 2 K). Source: FanGraphs only
- [HIGH] Cade Horton: Tommy John surgery, out ~16 months. Sources: FanGraphs + multiple prior pipeline runs
- [HIGH] Justin Steele: 60-day IL, multiple weeks from throwing, July return earliest. Sources: Cubbies Crib + FanGraphs
**Sources:**
- FanGraphs Injury Report (Boyd, Wicks, Steele, Horton)
- CBS Sports Injury Report (Boyd)
- Cubbies Crib (Steele update)

stories.title: Game 2 Hype — Ben Brown Takes the Mound at 6:15 PM CT
stories.angle: Pre-game energy. Cubs are 31-27 chasing both the Cardinals and Brewers. Brown (2.01 ERA) is the matchup advantage. Bear down.

x_posts.label: Post 6A
x_posts.text:
Game 2. Busch Stadium. 6:15 PM CT.

Ben Brown has a 2.01 ERA this season. The Cubs need him to be every bit of that tonight — 31-27 and chasing both the Cardinals and Brewers in the NL Central. Bear down. ⚾

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW
